bullets.text:
US scientists have confirmed that an El Ni
l
o event has officially begun, raising concerns about extreme weather patterns and higher global temperatures.
A deadly drone strike in Sudan targeted a funeral procession, with human rights groups accusing the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in the front-line city of el-Obeid.

An investigation has found that global brands, including Amazon and Sony, are likely using coltan sourced from supply chains controlled by M23 rebels, who are accused of atrocities in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
A century-old stormwater drain, nearly 2 meters thick, has been discovered beneath Queanbeyan's town center, causing budget overruns and delays for major roadworks.
The number of dogs euthanized by a regional Queensland council has more than tripled in recent years, surpassing figures from Australia's largest council by population size.
One Nation's predicted primary vote has reportedly surpassed both the Labor government and Coalition opposition for the first time, indicating a new level of popularity for the party.
SpaceX has completed the world's largest IPO, raising $75 billion and positioning Elon Musk to potentially become the world's first trillionaire.
Global markets rallied and oil prices fell as President Trump indicated a deal with Iran is close, easing concerns about conflict in the Middle East.
The Economist highlights that while China is innovative, its emerging industries may not be sufficient to offset the drag from its traditional economic sectors.
